calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Coffin for and with (Administrator) Jerry Chan.

Mr. Coffin reported gave background on the matter stating that the house had been underwater, that both parents died, that there was a short sale pending, that the real estate agents had failed to have Mr. Chan have letters issued, that Mr. Coffin was brought in to help Mr. Chan get letters which were issued, Mr. Chan signed off on the house, the real estate agents got their commission, Chase Bank was paid, Mr. Coffin thought that his limited scope representation was complete and was taken by surprise when he received notification from the examiner to come in. Mr. Coffin further stated that they determined that an Inventory & Appraisal was necessary, they had a referee appointed, that they had to petition for waiver of fees for filing the petition, that Chase was paid out of order and is now keeping the interest and that the estate received nothing. The Court asked if there was anything further to add. Mr. Coffin stated that he was asking for the service fees that were incurred for filing with File & Serve as well as the fees for hand delivery to Chase Bank but that he will absorb the costs if they get some relief. The Court took the accounting petition under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Boncato. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status For Filing Inventory And Appraisal And Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-02-2016 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status For Filing Inventory And Appraisal And Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-03-2016 Continued To Mar-02-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Coffin for (personal representative) Jerry Chan.

Mr. Coffin reported that Mr. Chan is downstairs filing the inventory & appraisal, that they need additional time to file the petition and that it may be a petition for termination due to lack of assets. The Court stated that the estate had real property and that it is not sure that a petition for termination would be appropriate. Mr. Coffin stated that the estate did not realize money from the sale but that he would file a petition for final distribution. The status on filing a petition for final distribution was continued to March 2, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status For Filing Inventory And Appraisal And Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-06-2016 Continued To Feb-03-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Coffin for (personal representative) Jerry Chan.

The Court stated that it needs the inventory & appraisal. Mr. Coffin stated that he has been in touch with the personal representative, that he was supposed to be present today, that Mr. Coffin only took on the case to help get the letters issued, that the title and real estate companies have been non-communicative and that he might be able to find out what happened with the house. The Court asked what the status of the house is. Mr. Coffin stated that a sale of the house in 2014 was found on Zillow, that he thinks that may be a further sale not the initial sale by the personal representative, explained that Mr. Chan had trouble getting letters issued, that he thinks Mr. Chan was under pressure from the agent to get letters at that time, that although Mr. Coffin was only to handle the case to the extent that letters were issued he agreed to follow through and that he is in touch with Mr. Chan and will talk to him regarding his responsibilities. The Court asked if it was necessary to issue an Order to Appear to Mr. Chan. Mr. Coffin stated that they are friendly, that it is not necessary and that a probate referee has been appointed. The Court stated that it will continue the matter, that Mr. Coffin is to make sure his client understands what is needed, that if the issues are not cleared up and an Inventory & Appraisal is not filed the court may consider suspension and asked in the information has been given to Warren Wong (probate referee). Mr. Coffin stated that it has not as he does not have Mr. Wong's information and that he may be able to obtain records from the title company. The status of filing an inventory & appraisal and petition for final distribution was continued to February 3, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Siu. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status For Filing Inventory And Appraisal And Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-09-2015 Continued To Jan-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status For Filing Inventory And Appraisal And Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-25-2015 Continued To Dec-09-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status For Filing Inventory And Appraisal And Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-28-2015 Continued To Nov-25-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Coffin for (administrator) Jerry Chan.

The Court stated that it is looking for an Inventory & Appraisal as well as a petition for final distribution. Mr. Coffin stated that he will jump right on it, that there were tragic circumstances regarding the family, that he took this case on as pro bono, that he lost touch with the administrator but spoke with him yesterday, that he has been in contact with the Probate Examiner and that he needs a probate referee. The Court asked what was in the estate. Mr. Coffin stated that they had a house in San Francisco that was under water and sold and that he does not think there is anything in the estate. The Court confirmed that a referee would be necessary, that it may be difficult to do a valuation on a house that no longer exists, that current contact information for the administrator on file, that it will continue the matter but advised that if there is difficulty in closing they may want to file a petition regarding the status of the administration. Mr. Coffin stated that he is exceeding his scope of representation but does not see any other way to do this. The status of filing and Inventory & Appraisal and petition for final distribution was continued to November 25, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status For Filing Inventory And Appraisal And Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-23-2015 Continued To Oct-28-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-16-2012 Granted Per Written Order

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jun-04-2012 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Coffin for and with Jerry Chan.

Mr. Coffin stated that he was retained this morning, that he has a Substitution of Attorney, that he has complied with regard to the probate Examiner's requests and asked for 40-days to re-publish. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to July 16, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Order to Appear was taken off calendar.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-04-2012 Continued To Jul-16-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-07-2012 Continued To Jun-04-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jerry Chan in Propria Persona. Dan Coffin also present.

The Court informed Mr. Coffin that Mr. Chan is representing himself, stated that there were some procedural problems that have not been corrected, that notes were sent out by the Probate Examiner and that it was willing to grant one more continuance. The Court strongly suggested to Mr. Cahn that he hire an Attorney. The Petition for Letters of Administration and the Order to Appear were continued to June 4, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Clauzel.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar May-07-2012 Continued To Jun-04-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-16-2012 Continued To May-07-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Diane Burton (Interested Party) in Propria Persona.

No appearance was made by Petitioner Jerry Chan. The Petition for Letters Administrator was continued to May 7, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Petitioner Jerry Chan to appear on May 7, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear and why he has not corrected the issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
